statistical_analyses.why_needed: Stochastic optimization comparisons require independent runs and a predeclared test rule.
assumptions: Use paired nonparametric tests when paired stochastic runs are appropriate.
statistical_analyses.random_seed_policy: Declare and publish the seed list before execution.
statistical_analyses.significance_level: predeclare alpha before execution; do not claim significance in the plan
statistical_analyses.multiple_comparison_policy: Use correction or Friedman plus post-hoc tests when comparing many algorithms.
statistical_analyses.effect_size_policy: Report effect size or confidence interval when empirical data exist.
statistical_analyses.confidence_interval_policy: Use bootstrap confidence intervals where assumptions warrant.
statistical_analyses.reporting_format: Report per-instance summary, test statistic, adjusted decision, and limitations after real runs.
statistical_analyses.interpretation_rule: The plan defines how results would be interpreted; it does not report results.
limitations: No p-value or significance conclusion exists before experiments are run.

assumptions: Statistical choices must be checked against real data design.
